title: tephroseris flammea subsp. glabrifolia
intro: Tephroseris flammea subsp. glabrifolia, also known as the woolly hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Tephroseris flammea subsp. glabrifolia can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and some studies suggest that it may also have antioxidant and antiviral properties.
